description: Canonical Codex model-profile routing policy. Logical profiles map to concrete Codex CLI model IDs and reasoning-effort values. This file is the sole source of truth for model selection -- three-brain.md documents the routing rules in prose, scripts/ops/model-routing.ts and scripts/ops/codex-exec.ts are the only code paths permitted to resolve or validate a profile, and no other file may hardcode a concrete Codex model ID.

verified_against.verification_method: codex --version / codex exec --help / ~/.codex/models_cache.json model catalog inspected directly on the installed CLI -- concrete model slugs and supported_reasoning_levels are copied from that catalog, not guessed.

codex-terra-medium.description: Balanced agentic coding model for everyday work. Default profile for normal clear-scope T2 Codex lanes.

codex-sol-high.description: Frontier agentic coding model. Used for complex or higher-stakes Codex lanes that still pass through the full Rule 1 / Rule 6 guardrail chain in three-brain.md (Claude critique, runtime verification, human review before merge). This profile does not itself grant T1 merge authority or bypass any existing gate.

codex-sol-max.description: Maximum reasoning depth. Reserved for a stuck lane that has already failed under codex-sol-high, or an explicitly PM-authorized escalation. Must never be selected as a default profile -- selecting this profile always requires a documented override (authorized_by + reason) in the manifest's model_routing block.

codex-luna-low.description: Fast and affordable agentic coding model. Defined for future use but reserved/disabled -- no ratified use case exists yet. Resolution must fail closed with profile_disabled while enabled=false. Do not enable to manufacture work for this profile; enabling requires a separate ratified policy change.

legacy_compatibility.description: Manifests created by ops:lane-start before this policy shipped have no model_routing block. scripts/ops/codex-exec.ts detects this by the field's absence (never by a schema_version bump -- the lane manifest schema_version stays 1) and resolves default_profile at execution time only. This resolution is never written back into the historical manifest; it is recorded solely in that run's evidence output (legacy_compatibility_used: true) and printed as a visible stderr warning.
